reforms.description: The Australian government plans to establish a medical research fund worth AUD 20 billion, utilizing savings from various health-related measures in the 2014-15 budget. This initiative aims to enhance funding for health-related research initiatives, thereby supporting innovation in the medical sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for medical research is expected to lead to advancements in health innovation, improving overall health outcomes and economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational as of 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant medical research fund represents a major structural change in funding for health-related research.
reforms.source_quote: The current government plans a highly targeted scheme to accumulate a medical research fund worth AUD 20 billion.

reforms.description: The South Australian government intends to expand its innovation voucher programme as part of a broader plan to help the economy following the announced closure of car assembly plants, aiming to encourage collaboration between industry and researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding the innovation voucher programme is expected to foster collaboration between industry and researchers, leading to increased innovation and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been expanded and is currently operational as of 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the innovation voucher programme represents a significant but narrower change aimed at enhancing innovation support.
reforms.source_quote: The South Australian government intends to expand its voucher programme as part of a broader plan to help the economy following the announced closure of car assembly plants.
